after.description:
Value to start iterating from.

Should be a value returned in the `pagination.next_page_id` field of a response.
after.name: after

limit.description: How many item to return on each page
limit.name: limit

400.description: Provided parameters or payload is malformed or invalid

403.description: The provided credentials are missing the required permissions for the requested action

404.description: The requested resource does not exist

409.description: The resource being created conflicts with an existing resource

422.description: The requested action is not possible on the requested resource

has_more.description: Whether there are more elements to iteration through

next_page_id.description: Value to use in pagination filter to request for the next page

decisions_created.description: Number of decisions that were created by this batch execution

manual.description: Whether the batch was started manually (or through scheduling)

review_status.description: Manual arbitration status for the decision when the outcome was `block_and_review`

rules.description: Rules that were executed for this decision

error.description: Any error that occured while executing the rule

name.description: Name of the rule in the scenario version

score_modifier.description: Impact of this rule on the decision score if it hit

code.description: Defined, machine-readable identifier for the error

detail.description: Free-form objects providing details on the error

messages.description: List of human-readable descriptions for the error

name.description: Optional name for the referenced object, not all references will have it.

id.description: ID of the scenario to use when creating decisions

iteration_id.description: Internal ID for the version of the scenario

match_count.description: Number of matches for the query

partial.description: Whether there were more matches than considered within `match_count`

queries.description: Queries that were submitted to OpenSanctions with their IDs
List of query IDs this match matched with

ScreeningMatchPayload.description:
OpenSanctions entity data.

This is an object as defined by [OpenSanctions entity format](https://api.opensanctions.org/#tag/Data-access/operation/fetch_entity_entities__entity_id__get).
ScreeningMatchPayload.title: Screening match entity

info.description: Public API on decisions and data ingestion.
info.title: Marble decision engine API

get.description: List scheduled, ongoing and finished batch executions

parameters.description: List batch executions for a specific scenario
parameters.name: scenario_id

200.description: List of batch executions matching the filters

get.description:
List decisions according to a filter set.

Listing decisions is always time-boxed between the provided `start` and `end` dates. If those parameters are omitted, it defaults to the past 30 days. If the parameters are provided, their range cannot exceed 90 days.

parameters.description: Filter decisions created by a specific batch execution
parameters.name: batch_execution_id

parameters.description: Filter decisions added to a given case
parameters.name: case_id

parameters.description: Filter decisions having a specific outcome
parameters.name: outcome

parameters.description:
Filter decisions having a specific review status.

Can only be used if `outcome` if set to `block_and_review`.
parameters.name: review_status

parameters.description: Filter decisions related a a specific trigger's `object_id`.
parameters.name: trigger_object_id

parameters.description: Filter decisions with a given pivot object value
parameters.name: pivot_value

parameters.description:
Only display decisions created after this date.

If provided, one must also provide an `end` date.
parameters.name: start

parameters.description:
Only display decisions created before this date.

If provided, one must also provide a `start` date.
parameters.name: end

200.description: List of decisions

post.description: Create a decision by running a trigger object through a scenario

scenario_id.description: ID of the scenario to run

trigger_object.description: The object on which to make a decision

200.description: A decision was successfully created

post.description: Create decisions by running a trigger object through all eligible scenarios

trigger_object.description: The object on which to make a decision

trigger_object_type.description: Type of the trigger object that is passed in

200.description: Between 0 and N decisions were created
Details of decision

get.description: Retrieve screening result details for decision

200.description: List of screening results for the decision

post.description:
Snooze a rule for a pivot value.

Snooze a rule for the provided duration, for the pivot value the decision pertains to. The given decision must be part of a case for its rules to be snoozed.

duration.description:
Duration for which the snooze will take effect.

Must use Go's [`time.ParseDuration`](https://pkg.go.dev/time#example-ParseDuration) syntax (e.g. "1h", "10h30m", etc.).

Maximum value is 180 days ("4320h").

account_id.description: Account that sent or received the transaction

card_is_3ds.description: Flag indicates if the card transaction has been secured by 3DS

card_merchant_id.description: Merchant code of a card transaction

card_merchant_name.description: Merchant name provided by the card scheme

card_payment_country.description: country of the merchant, for card transaction only

card_payment_currency.description: ISO code of the currency used for a card transaction

card_payment_type.description: indicates how the card transaction was processed and validated

category.description: MCC code of a card transaction. Empty for transfers and direct debit

counterparty_bic.description: BIC of a counterparty. Used for transfers only

counterparty_iban.description: IBAN of a counterparty (sender or beneficiary), used for transfers only

created_at.description: date of creation of the transaction in the system

creditor_identifier.description: Information provided by the creditor when sending a Direct Debit

direction.description: direction of the transaction. Can be PAYIN or PAYOUT

is_recuring.description: Flag used for direct debit mandates, indicates if recurring

payment_method.description: type of transaction. Can be: TRANSFER, CARD, DIRECT_DEBIT, WALLET_TRANSFER

scheme.description: indicates the type of direct debit received. Can be either B2B or CORE

status.description: status of the transaction. can be: VALIDATED, PENDING or CANCELED

value.description: Amount of the transaction, in EUR cents, no decimals

get.description: Retrieve a raw OpenSanctions entity from its ID.

200.description: Entity data as returned by OpenSanctions

post.description: Set the review status of a screening match

whitelist.description: Whether to whitelist the "no_hit" status to prevent triggering the match in the future

200.description: Match content reflecting the new status

post.description: Search for sanctioned entities outside of the context of a decision.

200.description: Screening result from the search

delete.description:
Delete a previously whitelisted entity, a match from the search term to the entity ID will subsequently trigger alerts.

If only `entity_id` is provided, all whitelist entries for that entity will be removed, regardless of which counterparty term triggers it.

counterparty.description: The unique counterparty identifier that should be whitelisted when yielding the associated entity ID

entity_id.description: The OpenSanctions entity ID to whitelist

post.description:
Add a set of search term and entity ID that will not set off a screening alert in the future.

This endpoint does not verify that the provided entity ID matches an **actual** entity on an OpenSanctions list. It is the responsibility of the caller to make sure they whitelist the correct entity ID for their needs.

counterparty.description: The unique counterparty identifier that should be whitelisted when yielding the associated entity ID

entity_id.description: The OpenSanctions entity ID to whitelist

post.description:
Search for whitelisted entities by OpenSanctions entity ID and/or unique counterparty identifier.

At least one of `counterparty` or `entity_id` is required to perform a search.

counterparty.description: The unique counterparty identifier to search for

entity_id.description: The OpenSanctions entity ID to search for

200.description: List of whitelist entries for the requested objects

post.description:
Replace a screening result with refined results.

This endpoint will replace the provided screening result with one containing the result of the included query.

200.description: Refined screening result for the decision

post.description:
Retrieve screening result without persisting them.

This endpoint will **not** replace the provided screening result, but allow to preview the results as executed in the original decision's context (using its unique counterparty ID and eligible whitelisted entries).

200.description: Screening results from the search
